name: Music Assistant
description: Music Assistant is a free, open-source media library manager that connects streaming services and local music to a wide range of players. This script deploys the official Docker server in an LXC container with host networking for player discovery.

notes.text: Requires Docker with host networking inside the LXC. Enable nesting and keyctl (defaults in this script).
Music Assistant developers recommend the Home Assistant add-on. This is an unofficial Docker LXC install path.

Web UI: http://<container-ip>:8095. Music Assistant requires layer-2 network access — MA, players, and Home Assistant should be on the same flat network or VLAN. Host networking is mandatory for the Docker container.

Update an existing container: run `update` inside the LXC, or `pct exec <CTID> -- update` from the Proxmox host. Interactive update shows Silent/Verbose/Cancel, then Update Music Assistant or Remove Unused Images. Unattended (`PHS_SILENT=1` or non-TTY) runs a silent app update.

If host-network Docker fails on an unprivileged LXC, try privileged mode: `var_unprivileged=0 bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oraad/proxmox-scripts/main/ct/musicassistant.sh)"`
Alpine template: `pveam update` then `pveam available --section system | grep alpine-3.24`. If 3.24 is unavailable, use `var_version=3.23`.

Optional local music library: during Advanced install you can enter a host path (bind-mounted to `/media` in the LXC). Unattended: `var_media_path=/path/on/host bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oraad/proxmox-scripts/main/ct/musicassistant.sh)"`. Unprivileged bind mounts may require correct host directory ownership or `var_unprivileged=0`.
